question_text: A wooden block floats on water, while a steel ball sinks. Which statement best explains this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: Wood is lighter than steel, so it floats.

options.label: B
options.text: Wood has a lower density than water, while steel has a higher density than water.

options.label: C
options.text: Wood is larger than steel, so it has more volume.

options.label: D
options.text: Steel is a metal and metals always sink in water.

explanation: Objects float when their density is less than the density of the liquid they are in, and sink when their density is greater. Wood (density ≈ 0.6 g/cm³) floats because it is less dense than water (1 g/cm³), while steel (density ≈ 7.8 g/cm³) sinks because it is denser than water.
